machinery_genes.quote: This is particularly evident in the virB2 and virB6 which are postulated to encode the secretion channel and proteins exposed on the bacterial surface.
machinery_genes.note: A. phagocytophilum VirB/D4 T4SS. virB2 is multi-copy (8-15 paralogs across strains; 8 in the HZ strain). Named as a periplasmic/outer-membrane channel subunit ('Periplasmic/outer membrane channel subunits: VirB2, VirB7 and VirB9') and the gene residing immediately downstream of the VirB4 ATPase ('encoding pilin').

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane subunit, pilus assembly / substrate translocation
machinery_genes.quote: VirB3 has been linked in [Agrobacterium] with pilus assembly and substrate translocation [,]. It is absolutely conserved between strains with no amino acid changes and conforms to the typical VirB3 structure.
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Inner membrane channel/scaffold subunits: VirB3, VirB6, VirB8 and VirB10'. Genus name was stripped from the PMC full-text extraction (rendered as '.'); restored in brackets.

machinery_genes.quote: Energetic subunits: VirB4 and VirB11. ATPases are typically used in T4SS to energize substrate transfer and have been found in every T4SS described.
machinery_genes.note: VirB4-2 is the energetic subunit, well conserved between strains. Encoded upstream of the virB2 pilin paralogs.

machinery_genes.quote: This is particularly evident in the virB2 and virB6 which are postulated to encode the secretion channel and proteins exposed on the bacterial surface.
machinery_genes.note: Four VirB6 paralogs (VirB6-1..VirB6-4) in a single locus; VirB6-4 is unusually large/repetitive (>500 kDa). Section 'Inner membrane channel/scaffold subunits: VirB3, VirB6, VirB8 and VirB10'.

machinery_genes.quote: VirB7 subunits are typically small lipoproteins that may stabilize VirB9 [,]. In [A. phagocytophilum] strains a putative VirB7 is absolutely conserved between strains and may be lipid modified through an N-terminal cysteine on the mature molecule.
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Periplasmic/outer membrane channel subunits: VirB2, VirB7 and VirB9'. Genus name stripped in PMC extraction; restored in brackets.

machinery_genes.quote: VirB8, proposed to function as a[n] [assembly] factor during the assembly of T4SS [,], is also well conserved, particularly VirB8-1 in the polycistronic transcription locus (one amino acid change between all strains).
machinery_genes.note: VirB8-1 is encoded on the polycistronic virB8-virD4 transcript. Section 'Inner membrane channel/scaffold subunits: VirB3, VirB6, VirB8 and VirB10'.

machinery_genes.quote: VirB9-1, which is encoded on the polycistronic [virB8-virD4] transcript [], has a strongly predicted signal peptide and two transmembrane helices.
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Periplasmic/outer membrane channel subunits: VirB2, VirB7 and VirB9'. C-terminal region forms part of the surface-exposed OM channel. Genus/operon name stripped in PMC extraction; restored in brackets.

machinery_genes.quote: VirB10, proposed to function as a scaffold across the entire cell envelope [], is also generally well-conserved... all [A. phagocytophilum] VirB10s... have two strongly predicted transmembrane domains, which supports their function as membrane scaffolding subunits in these organisms.
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Inner membrane channel/scaffold subunits: VirB3, VirB6, VirB8 and VirB10'. Genus name stripped in PMC extraction; restored in brackets.

machinery_genes.quote: The other energetic subunit, VirB11, was also well-conserved between strains (6 amino acid substitutions between [NorLamb-V1] and [HZ]; data not shown).
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Energetic subunits: VirB4 and VirB11'. Strain abbreviations restored in brackets.

machinery_genes.quote: Type 4 coupling proteins such as VirD4 are ATPases that function in substrate recognition and translocation using the T4SS... The [A. phagocytophilum] VirD4s conform somewhat to this stereotype with three strongly predicted N-terminal transmembrane segments.
machinery_genes.note: Section 'Type 4 coupling protein: VirD4'. Independently confirmed as the coupling protein that recognises the C-terminal positively charged residues of AnkA and Ats-1 (Rikihisa 2010 review, PMID 20670295). Genus name stripped in PMC extraction; restored in brackets.

notes: System = the A. phagocytophilum VirB/D4 type-IVA secretion system (Rickettsiales archetype). Apparatus genes taken from Al-Khedery et al 2012 (BMC Genomics, PMID 23190684), which sequenced and described the vir loci across 8 A. phagocytophilum strains INCLUDING the human HZ strain (resequenced here to correct the original virB6 locus). The vir genes are distributed in three conserved split clusters (the brief notes the family uses virB1-11 + virD4 directly). The HZ strain has the FULL canonical Rickettsiales VirB/D4 component set: VirB2, VirB3, VirB4, VirB6, VirB7, VirB8, VirB9, VirB10, VirB11, VirD4. VirB1 (lytic transglycosylase) and VirB5 (minor pilin) are absent from the A. phagocytophilum T4SS, consistent with the reduced Rickettsiales architecture, and were verified absent in this paper's full text; deliberately not listed. Several components are multi-copy in this organism: virB2 (8 paralogs in HZ, up to 15 in ruminant strains), virB6 (4 paralogs), virB4 and virB9 have copies; the gene-level entries above record the family, not individual paralog locus_tags (locus/coordinate resolution is a separate scripted step). Quotes are verbatim from the PMC full text; the genus/strain/operon proper names were stripped to '.' during full-text extraction and are restored in [brackets] where needed for readability (the gene names themselves are unaltered). The instance's effectors (AnkA = APH_0740, Ats-1, plus the other curated APH effectors) and the coupling-protein-recognised substrates are NOT listed as machinery. VirD4, although it recognises substrates, is the apparatus coupling protein and is correctly counted as machinery. No transcriptional regulators included.
